name: zvideo_dd_unlock_directdraw_surface
description: zVideo_dd::UnlockDirectDrawSurface unlocks a DirectDraw surface, restores and retries once when the surface is lost, and reports unrecovered DirectDraw errors.
known_limits: VC6 cl 12.00.8168 x86 /O2 /Ob0 /Zp4 /FAcs COFF byte comparison for ?UnlockDirectDrawSurface@zVideo_dd@@YIHPAUIDirectDrawSurface3@@@Z still fails: the compiler emits a duplicated second Unlock call after successful Restore instead of the original loop branch back to the first Unlock block. The reviewed run reported 47 unmasked mismatches, 4 relocation-masked bytes, and 2 trailing VC6 NOP bytes trimmed.
functional_evidence: Binary Ninja assembly for 0x4a80c0 calls IDirectDrawSurface3::Unlock through vtable offset 0x80 with a null argument, returns zero on DD_OK, calls Restore through vtable offset 0x6c on DDERR_SURFACELOST, retries Unlock when Restore succeeds, and reports other HRESULT failures with zVideo_dd::ReportError using zvid_dd.c line 0x1d7 before returning 0x5a56ffff.

Binary Ninja decompilation for 0x4a80c0 has no warnings and matches the source loop: unlock, optional restore on DDERR_SURFACELOST, retry on successful restore, otherwise report the HRESULT and return failure.

The native smoke installs a fake IDirectDrawSurface3 vtable and verifies the direct DD_OK unlock path plus the DDERR_SURFACELOST, Restore DD_OK, retry Unlock DD_OK path, including null Unlock arguments and call counts.
